commit.message:
fix(supervisor): send bridge heartbeat before terminal for fast workers

Workers that complete without emitting heartbeat events left the lease
in STARTING state. The terminal heartbeat then failed because
STARTING → COMPLETING is not a valid transition (only RUNNING/HEARTBEATING
→ COMPLETING is allowed).

Now the supervisor sends a non-terminal bridge heartbeat before the
terminal one. This advances the lease STARTING → RUNNING, then the
terminal heartbeat completes RUNNING → COMPLETING. When the lease is
already past STARTING (from regular stream heartbeats), the bridge
heartbeat is handled as a normal heartbeat progression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow (#154)

* chore: add rollback-release GitHub Actions workflow

Manual workflow_dispatch that rolls back the VPS to any previously
released image version. Accepts a version string (e.g. 0.12.4 or v0.12.4),
strips the leading 'v' to match the Docker image tag convention, pulls
the pinned image on the VPS, retags it as :latest, and restarts via
docker compose — without touching .env or docker-compose.yml.

Shares the deploy-vps concurrency group to prevent concurrent
deploy/rollback races. Guarded to main branch only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add deploy-vps concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml

The rollback workflow used group: deploy-vps but no existing deploy
workflow shared that group, so concurrent deploy + rollback runs were
not actually blocked.

Adding the same concurrency group to _deploy-vps.yml (the reusable
workflow called by both deploy.yml and release.yml) means all VPS
deployments now share the group with rollback.yml, enforcing real
mutual exclusion.

Addresses PR review comment: concurrency group not shared with existing deploy workflows

commit.message:
fix(supervisor): transition lease LEASED → STARTING before heartbeat loop

The worker supervisor now transitions the lease from LEASED to STARTING
after spawning the worker process, before the heartbeat streaming loop
begins. Previously the lease stayed in LEASED state and heartbeats were
rejected because the heartbeat service only accepts STARTING, RUNNING,
or HEARTBEATING states.

Adds LeaseTransitionerPort to WorkerSupervisorDependencies (optional,
for backward compatibility) and wires the TransitionService adapter
in the automation runtime.

commit.message:
fix: add --project flag to uv entrypoint for GitHub Actions compatibility (#22)

GitHub Actions Docker container runner overrides the working directory
to /github/workspace via --workdir. This causes uv run to not find the
pyproject.toml at /action/workspace, so it falls back to system Python
which has no packages installed, resulting in:

  ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'github3'

Adding --project /action/workspace ensures uv always discovers the
project and its .venv regardless of the runtime working directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix blend compositing and ColorTransform int16 overflow

Replace blend mode propagation with intermediate canvas approach:
children with non-zero blend are composited internally first (normal
blend), then the result is applied with the parent's blend mode. This
prevents alpha loss from additive sub-fragments.

Fix critical ColorTransform bug: int16 overflow during multiplication
(e.g. 150 * 255 = 38250 > 32767). Changed to int32 for intermediate
calculations. This produced incorrect dark pixels in color-transformed
sprites.

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ghostty): pre-warm portal.Desktop and Flatpak synchronously

The previous wrapper fired StartServiceByName for org.freedesktop.Flatpak
in the background (&), which raced with ghostty's own D-Bus calls — ghostty
could still stall waiting for a cold service.

Change to synchronous pre-warm of both:
  - org.freedesktop.portal.Desktop  (xdg-desktop-portal, used for
    GlobalShortcuts and portal settings on every startup)
  - org.freedesktop.Flatpak         (flatpak-session-helper, used for
    FlatpakHostCommand to spawn shells on the host)

When both services are already running each gdbus call returns in <10 ms.
When cold they block for the activation duration (~1-3 s each) so ghostty
starts with both services provably ready, eliminating any internal timeout.

Also update GOTCHAS.md with corrected dual-service root cause, improved
diagnostics, and a note about the host-level portal backend conflict case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(metrics): add cross-CLI token usage tracking and dashboard enhancements (#16)

# Pull request description

## Describe your changes

- **New `CLIUsageProvider`**: reads local session data from GitHub
Copilot (`~/.copilot/session-state/*/events.jsonl`), Gemini CLI
(`~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats/session-*.json`), and Claude Code
(`~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl`) to surface real per-CLI token
consumption alongside RTK savings.
- **Dashboard redesign**: replaced the two old charts (doughnut + pie)
with a single donut chart showing RTK Saved + per-CLI token slices, and
a taller multi-line daily chart with one line per CLI plus RTK
input/output/saved.
- **New summary cards**: CLI Tool Calls (Copilot
`tool.execution_complete`, Gemini `toolCalls`, Claude `tool_use`
blocks), CLI Total (with per-CLI sublabel), and True Savings % (`saved /
(CLI total + RTK input)`).
- **Gemini token inflation fix**: uses `output + thoughts` per message
instead of `total`, avoiding ~300× overcounting from cumulative context
re-sent each turn.
- **Overlap filtering**: CLI tokens are only counted on days where RTK
also has data for an apples-to-apples savings comparison.
- **Removed status bar item** and all associated polling code from
`extension.ts`.
- **README updated** with dashboard screenshot, supported CLIs table,
and revised feature descriptions.

commit.message:
fix: resilient usage display under API rate limiting (#193)

* fix: resilient usage display under API rate limiting

The Anthropic usage API rate-limits to ~1 call per 5 minutes. With the
previous 60s cache TTL, 4 out of 5 API calls returned 429, causing the
HUD to permanently display "(429)" instead of actual usage data.

Three-layer fix:
- Increase cache TTL from 60s to 5 minutes to match rate limit window
- Preserve lastGoodData in cache across rate-limited periods so the HUD
  always shows the best available data instead of errors
- Exponential backoff (60s→120s→240s→5min cap) with Retry-After header
  support for consecutive 429 responses

Also show "syncing..." instead of raw HTTP status on first-run rate limit.

* Update usage-api.ts

image.save() output is accumulated fully in memory (chunks + b''.join(...)). Docker save tars for real images can be multiple GB, so this can cause high memory usage or OOM. Consider streaming to a temporary file (e.g., tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile / TemporaryFile) and opening the tar from that file instead of buffering the entire archive in RAM.

commit.message:
Step 2c/2d: Add example/hello, Window.SetLayout, layoutAdapter, bump to v0.2.0

- Add example/hello/main.go: minimal working application using only the
  public wain API (no internal/ imports). Creates a window, builds a
  Column layout with a Label and Button, handles OnClick, falls back to
  headless output when no display server is available.

- Add Window.SetLayout(PublicWidget) to app.go: public convenience method
  that wraps a PublicWidget tree in a layoutAdapter and passes it to
  SetRootWidget. This closes the gap where app code could not attach
  a Column/Row/etc. to a Window without accessing internal types.

- Add layoutAdapter to publicwidget.go: bridges PublicWidget → Widget
  (the interface used by RenderBridge and EventDispatcher). Implements
  Children() by type-asserting to Container and wrapping each child
  recursively, Contains() from widget bounds, and all event forwarding
  methods (HandlePointer, HandleKey, HandleTouch, SetFocused, IsFocused).

- Bump version references from v0.1.0 to v0.2.0 in README.md and
  RELEASE.md; add 'unstable but usable' API stability notice.
